key_insight: In older adults with mild traumatic brain injury, 6-month recovery varied strongly by age and pre-injury cognitive status, with worse outcomes in the oldest-old and those with dementia.
clinical_relevance: GOSE may be a useful 6-month functional endpoint for geriatric mTBI studies, but outcomes should be interpreted alongside cognitive baseline and complementary disability measures.
method: Prospective cohort at two U.S. level I trauma centers
Older adults (65+) presenting within 72 hours of mTBI and receiving head CT
GOSE assessed at 2 weeks and 6 months, with weighting to reduce attrition bias
evidence_signal: Among 253 participants, 14% died by 6 months, 73% reached home independence, and 17% achieved complete recovery; GOSE captured more reliable change than ADL or FAQ in this sample.
watch_item: Single-center pilot and later two-center enrollment, plus substantial unknown pre-injury cognitive status, may limit generalizability and endpoint interpretation.
paper_title: 6-Month Recovery after Mild Traumatic Brain Injury in Older Adults: A TRACK-GERI Study
